svg.text: MN 5 — ANAṄGAṆA SUTTA
Blemish Free
FOUR KINDS OF PERSONS · CATTĀRO PUGGALĀ
Doesn't Clearly Understand · nappajānāti
Clearly Understands · yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti
With Blemish · sāṅgaṇa
Blemish-Free · anaṅgaṇa
A Person Who Doesn't Recognize Their Blemish
is like a dirty bronze bowl hidden in a dusty corner,
left to gather more stains.
They will not generate the aspiration or exert effort to abandon that blemish
na chandaṁ janessati na vāyamissati tassaṅgaṇassa pahānāya
As a result, they will die with passion, aversion, and delusion—a defiled mind
sarāgo sadoso samoho saṅkiliṭṭhacitto kālaṁ karissati
A Person Who Recognizes Their Blemish
is like a dirty bronze bowl that is used,
washed, and polished clean.
They will generate the aspiration and exert effort to abandon that blemish
chandaṁ janessati vāyamissati tassaṅgaṇassa pahānāya
They will die without passion, aversion, and delusion—with an undefiled mind
arāgo adoso amoho asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto kālaṁ karissati
A Blemish-Free Person Who Doesn't Recognize It
is like a clean bronze bowl hidden in a dusty corner,
left to gather stains and become defiled.
Attending to the sign of the beautiful, passion will invade their mind
subhanimittaṁ manasi karissati rāgo cittaṁ anuddhaṁsessati
They will die with passion, aversion, and delusion—with a defiled mind
sarāgo sadoso samoho saṅkiliṭṭhacitto kālaṁ karissati
A Blemish-Free Person Who Recognizes It
is like a clean bronze bowl that is used,
washed, and kept perfectly pure.
Not attending to the sign of the beautiful, passion will not invade their mind
subhanimittaṁ na manasi karissati rāgo cittaṁ nānuddhaṁsessati
They will die without passion, aversion, and delusion—with an undefiled mind
arāgo adoso amoho asaṅkiliṭṭhacitto kālaṁ karissati
WHAT IS A BLEMISH?
Blemish · aṅgaṇa
Harmful, unwholesome [qualities] within the realm of desire
pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ icchāvacarānaṁ adhivacanaṁ
Wish arises
icchā uppajjeyya
Opposite occurs
Becomes angry and displeased
Both that anger and displeasure
are a blemish (aṅgaṇa)
UNWHOLESOME WISHES · PĀPAKĀ AKUSALĀ ICCHĀVACARĀ
Wish arises · icchā uppajjeyya
Concealing Wrongdoing
"May others not know of my offense (āpatti)"
"May I be admonished privately (anuraho), not amidst the Saṅgha"
"May only a peer (sappaṭipuggalo) correct me, not a lesser"
Desire for Status
"May the teacher question me before others"
"May I lead the alms procession to the village"
"May I receive the best seat, water, and food"
"May I give the blessing after meals"
Desire to Teach
"May I be the one to teach the Dhamma"
To monks · nuns · laymen · laywomen
Desire for Honor
"May others honor, respect, revere, and venerate me"
From monks · nuns · laymen · laywomen
Desire for the Finest Requisites · paṇītā paccayā
Robes · cīvara
Alms food · piṇḍapāta
Lodging · senāsana
Medicine · bhesajja
Opposite occurs
Becomes angry and displeased
kupito hoti appatīto
Both that anger and displeasure
are a blemish · aṅgaṇaṁ
APPEARANCE VS REALITY — ANOTHER BRONZE BOWL SIMILE
A forest dweller with harmful & unwholesome qualities not given up
āraññiko … pāpakā akusalā icchāvacarā appahīnā
is like a carcass that is carefully covered in a bowl
Just the sight of it inspires such
loathing (amanāpatā), revulsion (pāṭikulyatā), and repulsion (jegucchatā)
They are neither honored nor respected · na sakkaronti na garuṁ karonti
A village dweller with harmful & unwholesome qualities abandoned
gāmantavihārī … pāpakā akusalā icchāvacarā pahīnā
is like fine food that is carefully covered in a bowl
Just the sight of it inspires such
pleasantness (manāpatā), appetite (appāṭikulyatā), and relish (ajegucchatā)
They are honored and respected · sakkaronti garuṁ karonti
"If these harmful and unwholesome mental qualities within the realm of desire
are seen and heard to be abandoned in any one …
his fellows in the spiritual life honor, respect, revere, and venerate him."
yassa kassaci bhikkhuno ime pāpakā akusalā icchāvacarā pahīnā dissanti ceva sūyanti ca …
sabrahmacārī sakkaronti garuṁ karonti mānenti pūjenti
Te hi tassa āyasmato pāpakā akusalā icchāvacarā pahīnā dissanti ceva sūyanti cā
